verses.text: If a man receives wisdom's words, cries for understanding, and searches for her 'as for hid treasures,' then he shall 'understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God.' Wisdom is won by earnest seeking. The treasure of knowing God rewards those who dig for it.

If a man receives wisdom's words, cries for understanding, and searches for her 'as for hid treasures,' then he shall 'understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God.' Wisdom is won by earnest seeking. The treasure of knowing God rewards those who dig for it.
'For the LORD giveth wisdom,' laying up sound counsel for the upright and guarding their way. Wisdom is His gift. He grants discernment of what is right to those who walk with Him.
'For the LORD giveth wisdom,' laying up sound counsel for the upright and guarding their way. Wisdom is His gift. He grants discernment of what is right to those who walk with Him.
'For the LORD giveth wisdom,' laying up sound counsel for the upright and guarding their way. Wisdom is His gift. He grants discernment of what is right to those who walk with Him.
'For the LORD giveth wisdom,' laying up sound counsel for the upright and guarding their way. Wisdom is His gift. He grants discernment of what is right to those who walk with Him.

When wisdom enters the heart, discretion preserves a man, delivering him from the way of the evil and froward. Inward wisdom is a guard. A heart filled with truth is kept from the snares of the wicked.

Such men leave the paths of uprightness to walk in darkness, rejoicing to do evil. They love crookedness for its own sake. There is a delight in sin that hardens those who give themselves to it.

Wisdom also delivers from 'the strange woman,' whose flattery leads to death, for 'her house inclineth unto death.' Her allure is deadly. The path of forbidden pleasure ends in ruin no one returns from.

So a man may walk in the way of good men, for 'the upright shall dwell in the land,' but the wicked shall be cut off from it. Wisdom keeps us in the right road. The end of righteousness is to abide; the end of wickedness is to be uprooted.
